summary:
The prevalence of pit and fissure caries can be assessed using ICDAS due to its comprehensive approach to detecting dental cavities, especially those located in pits and fissures which are harder to see or access during routine examinations. This system provides a standardized method that aligns well with contemporary dental practice guidelines.
To refine this summary further while keeping within two concise sentences as requested by your instructions, here’s an adjusted version focusing only on critical elements related directly back towards answering how such prevalences would typically get quantified clinically today without extraneous details about specific options provided earlier since they were not part of our discussion context anyway:
